meaning: adj.国事的,团体事务的,实际的,注重实效的
examples: All communication systems serve a purpose, a pragmatic function of some sort.
Knowledge of chronobiological patterns can have many pragmatic implications for our day-to-day lives.
One reason for the striking upsurge of social psychology in the United States lies in the pragmatic tradition of this country.
When she faces criticism, the knock is she's too pragmatic as often as too pushy.
We ought to take the same pragmatic approach to debates over monetary policy and broadband policy.
